name: ICICI Prudential Nifty 10yr Benchmark G-Sec ETF

index.name: Nifty 10yr Benchmark G-Sec Index
index.description: Government of India Securities (G-Secs) represent pure sovereign credit — zero default risk. The 10-year benchmark G-Sec is RBI's primary policy transmission instrument. In June 2024, India's 10-year G-Sec inclusion in JP Morgan GBI-EM (Global Bond Index) triggered ~$25 billion passive foreign inflows, creating structural demand. Investors benefit from dual returns: (1) coupon income (7-8% annualized), (2) capital appreciation when RBI cuts rates (inverse relationship: rate ↓ → price ↑). Ideal for retirees seeking stable income, long-term wealth preservation, and tax efficiency (indexation benefit on long-term gains). G-Secs suitable for laddering strategy where 5-year, 10-year and 20-year bonds provide staggered maturity. RBI Open Market Operations use benchmark G-Secs as primary instrument, making them most liquid debt securities globally. During Fed rate hike cycles, G-Secs suffer from capital losses (rate ↑ → price ↓), but during easing cycles (post-2025 expected), substantial capital gains likely.

holdings.name: GoI 7.10% 2034
holdings.description: Current on-the-run 10-year Government of India Security. Coupon 7.10%, maturing 2034. Included in JP Morgan GBI-EM Global Diversified index since June 2024 — triggers ~$25 billion in passive foreign inflows. RBI Open Market Operations use this as primary instrument. Zero credit risk, backed by sovereign guarantee.

name: Nippon India ETF Nifty 5yr Benchmark G-Sec

index.name: Nifty 5yr Benchmark G-Sec Index
index.description: Tracks the on-the-run 5-year Government of India Security. Shorter duration than the 10yr ETF — less interest rate sensitivity. Suitable for investors wanting sovereign safety with moderate duration. Yield slightly below 10yr providing the normal upward-sloping yield curve exposure.

holdings.name: GoI 7.02% 2029
holdings.description: Current on-the-run 5-year Government of India Security. Coupon 7.02%, maturing 2029. Shorter duration means less price volatility for same change in yield. Preferred by conservative investors wanting G-Sec exposure without duration risk. Actively traded on NDS-OM platform by banks and PDs.

name: Nippon India ETF Nifty 8-13 yr G-Sec Long Term Gilt

index.name: NIFTY 8-13 yr G-Sec Index
index.description: Nippon India ETF Nifty 8-13 yr G-Sec Long Term Gilt tracks the NIFTY 8-13 yr G-Sec Index index.

holdings.name: 7.26% GOI 2033
holdings.description: Current 10-year benchmark Government of India bond. Most liquid G-Sec by trading volume on NDS-OM. Modified duration ~7.2 years. Yield ~7.10-7.30%; widely used by FPIs and domestic institutions to gauge interest rate environment.

holdings.name: 7.18% GOI 2033
holdings.description: 10-year GOI bond from prior issuance cycle. Lower coupon but similar maturity to benchmark. Both bonds deliver identical sovereign credit quality; minor yield differences reflect issuance cycle and liquidity.

holdings.name: 7.40% GOI 2035
holdings.description: 12-year GOI bond offering slightly higher yield than 10-year benchmark from steeper long end of yield curve. Modified duration ~8 years. Suitable for ALM purposes by insurance and pension funds.

holdings.name: 6.54% GOI 2032
holdings.description: 9-year G-Sec with below-par coupon reflecting low-rate issuance period (2021-22). Lower coupon = higher duration, amplifying sensitivity to rate changes — beneficial when rates fall.

holdings.name: 7.30% GOI 2053
holdings.description: 30-year ultra-long G-Sec included in long-term gilt indices. Modified duration ~16 years. Key holding for insurance and pension liabilities ALM. High convexity benefits from rate cut cycles.

name: NIPPON INDIA ETF NIFTY 5 YR BENCHMARK GSEC

index.name: Nifty 5 yr Benchmark G-Sec Index
index.description: NIPPON INDIA ETF NIFTY 5 YR BENCHMARK GSEC tracks the Nifty 5 yr Benchmark G-Sec Index index.

holdings.name: 7.37% GOI 2028
holdings.description: Government of India bond maturing in 2028 with 7.37% semi-annual coupon. Benchmark 5-year security. Issued via RBI auctions; eligible as SLR security. Modified duration ~3.8 years; lower interest-rate sensitivity than long bonds.

holdings.name: 7.17% GOI 2028
holdings.description: 5-year GOI benchmark security with 7.17% coupon. Issued to finance fiscal deficit. Zero default risk; repayment guaranteed by Government of India.

holdings.name: 7.26% GOI 2029
holdings.description: GOI bond maturing 2029. Part of standard G-Sec issuance calendar for medium-duration sovereign exposure. Actively traded on NDS-OM.

holdings.name: 6.79% GOI 2027
holdings.description: 3-year residual maturity GOI bond. Lower coupon reflects issuance period of lower rates. Duration ~2.5 years, lower interest rate risk.

holdings.name: 7.06% GOI 2028
holdings.description: Medium-term GOI security. All G-Secs are backed by full faith and credit of the Union of India and RBI guarantees payment on due dates.
